name: OMNIPRO 220
description: A multiprocess welding system manufactured by Vulcan

name: Read manual before using product
description: Operator must read the Owner's Manual & Safety Instructions before using the OMNIPRO 220 to prevent serious injury

name: Save this manual
description: Keep the manual for safety warnings, precautions, assembly, operating, inspection, maintenance and cleaning procedures. Write the product Date Code in the back of the manual. Keep manual and receipt in a safe and dry place for future reference.

name: Inspect product upon delivery
description: When unpacking, make sure that the product is intact and undamaged. If any parts are missing or broken, call 1-800-444-3353 as soon as possible.

name: OMNIPRO 220 Multiprocess Welding System
description: Product image showing the OMNIPRO 220 welding machine with front control panel and side profile

name: MIG/Flux-Cored Wire Welding
description: A welding process using MIG or Flux-Cored wire electrode with shielding gas protection

name: TIG/Stick Welding
description: A welding process using TIG or Stick electrode methods

name: Read all Safety warnings and instructions
description: Operator must read and follow all safety warnings and instructions to avoid electric shock, fire, and serious injury

name: Review Material Safety Data Sheets and ANSI Z49.1
description: Before use, read and understand manufacturer's instructions, Material Safety Data Sheets (MSDS's), employer's Safety practices, and ANSI Z49.1

name: Keep children and bystanders away while operating
description: Keep out of reach of children and keep children and bystanders away while operating the welder

name: Place welder on stable location before use
description: Place the welder on a stable location before use to prevent falling, which may result in severe injury, electric shock, or fire

name: Keep proper footing and balance at all times
description: Do not overreach; keep proper footing and balance at all times to prevent falls and loss of control

name: Stay alert and do not operate while tired or under influence
description: Stay alert, watch what you are doing and use common sense when operating a welder. Do not use a welder while tired or under the influence of drugs, alcohol or medication. A moment of inattention while operating welders may result in serious personal injury

name: Avoid unintentional starting and be prepared before turning on
description: Avoid unintentional starting. Make sure you are prepared to begin work before turning on the Welder

name: Never leave Welder unattended while energized
description: Never leave the Welder unattended while energized. Turn power off if you have to leave

name: Operator must exercise common sense and caution
description: The warnings, precautions, and instructions discussed in this instruction manual cannot cover all possible conditions and situations that may occur. It must be understood by the operator that common sense and caution are factors which cannot be built into this product, but must be supplied by the operator

name: Exposure to welding or cutting exhaust fumes can increase risk of developing certain cancers
description: Inhalation hazard warning about toxic fumes from welding and plasma cutting processes, including risks of cancer of the larynx and lung cancer, heart disease, ulcers, reproductive damage, intestinal or stomach inflammation, kidney damage, and respiratory diseases

name: Use natural or forced air ventilation and wear NIOSH-approved respirator
description: Protective measure to reduce risk of developing illnesses from welding or plasma cutting exhaust fumes

name: Keep head out of fumes and do not breathe exhaust fumes
description: Direct instruction to avoid inhalation of welding and plasma cutting exhaust fumes

name: Use enough ventilation, exhaust at arc, or both, to keep fumes and gases from breathing zone
description: Control measure for fume and gas management during welding operations

name: Work in confined area only if well-ventilated, or while wearing air-supplied respirator
description: Safety requirement for confined space welding operations

name: Have recognized specialist in Industrial Hygiene or Environmental Services check operation and air quality
description: Requirement to consult with specialist and follow OSHA guidelines for Permissible Exposure Limits (PEL's) and American Conference of Governmental Industrial Hygienists recommendations for Threshold Limit Values (TLV's)

name: Do not use near degreasing or painting operations
description: Safety precaution to avoid hazardous interactions during welding

name: Wear ANSI-approved welding eye protection featuring at least a number 10 shade lens rating
description: Protection against arc rays that can injure eyes and burn skin

name: Wear leather leggings, fire resistant shoes or boots during use
description: Protective clothing to prevent catches and holds of molten metal or sparks

name: Do not wear pants with cuffs, shirts with open pockets, or any clothing that can catch and hold molten metal or sparks
description: Clothing restriction to prevent spark and molten metal entrapment

name: Keep clothing free of grease, oil, solvents, or any flammable substances
description: Measure to prevent ignition of clothing during welding operations

name: Wear dry, insulating gloves and protective clothing
description: Personal protective equipment required for arc ray safety

name: Wear an approved head covering to protect the head and neck
description: Approved head covering including aprons, capes, sleeves, shoulder covers, and bibs designed and approved for welding and cutting procedures

name: Wear an approved welding jacket or long sleeves to protect forearms from radiation burns
description: Protection against arc radiation exposure during welding operations

name: When welding/cutting overhead or in confined spaces, wear flame resistant ear plugs or ear muffs
description: Safety measure to keep sparks out of ears during overhead or confined space welding operations

name: Turn off, disconnect power, and discharge electrode to ground before setting down torch/electrode holder and before service
description: Safety precaution to prevent accidental electrical shock during equipment setup or maintenance

name: Do not touch energized electrical parts
description: Instruction to wear dry insulating gloves and avoid contact with electrode holder, electrode, and welding torch with bare hands

name: Connect to grounded, GFCI-protected power supply only
description: Requirement for electrical safety using proper grounding and circuit protection

name: Do not use near water or damp objects
description: Safety precaution to prevent electrical shock hazards in wet conditions

name: People with pacemakers should consult their physician(s) before use
description: Warning that electromagnetic fields in close proximity to heart pacemaker could cause interference or failure

name: Do not expose welders to rain or wet conditions
description: Precaution that water entering a welder will increase the risk of electric shock

name: Do not abuse the cord. Never use the cord for carrying, pulling or unplugging the welder
description: Instruction to keep cord away from heat, oil, sharp edges or moving parts as damaged or entangled cords increase the risk of electric shock

name: Do not use outdoors
description: Safety restriction to prevent outdoor use of the welding equipment

name: Insulate yourself from the workpiece and ground
description: Instruction to use nonflammable, dry insulating material if possible, or dry rubber mats, dry wood or plywood to cover full area of contact with work or ground

name: THIS WELDING MACHINE MUST BE CONNECTED TO POWER SOURCE IN ACCORDANCE WITH APPLICABLE ELECTRICAL CODES
description: Regulatory requirement for proper electrical connection of the welding machine

name: Clear away or protect flammable objects
description: Instruction to remove or make safe all combustible materials for a radius of 35 feet (10 meters) around the work area and use fire resistant material to cover or block all open doorways, windows, cracks, and other openings

name: Keep ABC-type fire extinguisher near work area and know how to use it
description: Requirement to maintain fire suppression equipment accessible during welding operations

name: Maintain a safe working environment
description: Instruction to keep work area well lit, ensure adequate surrounding workspace, and keep work area free of obstructions, grease, oil, trash, and other debris

name: Do not operate welders in atmospheres containing dangerously reactive or flammable liquids, gases, vapors, or dust
description: Warning that welders create sparks which may ignite flammable substances or make reactive fumes toxic; adequate ventilation required

name: If working on a metal wall, ceiling, etc., prevent ignition of combustibles on the other side
description: Instruction to move the combustibles to a safe location or designate someone to serve as a fire watch during and for at least one half hour after cutting is completed

name: Do not weld or cut on materials having a combustible coating or combustible internal structure
description: Prohibition without an approved method for eliminating the hazard, such as in walls or ceilings

name: Do not dispose of hot slag in containers holding combustible materials
description: Safety precaution to prevent fire from hot slag

name: After welding, make a thorough examination for evidence of fire
description: Instruction to be aware that easily visible smoke or flame may not be present for some time after the fire has started

name: Do not apply heat to a container that has held an unknown substance or a combustible material whose contents, when heated, can produce flammable or explosive vapors
description: Safety precaution to clean and purge containers before applying heat; vent closed containers including castings before preheating, welding, or cutting

name: Do not use the welder if the switch does not turn it on and off
description: Safety precaution stating that any welder that cannot be controlled with the switch is dangerous and must be repaired

name: Disconnect the plug from the power source before making any adjustments, changing accessories, or storing welders
description: Safety measure to reduce the risk of starting the welder accidentally

name: Prevent unintentional starting by ensuring the switch is in the off position before connecting to power source
description: Carrying or energizing welders with switch on invites accidents

name: Store idle welders out of the reach of children and do not allow persons unfamiliar with the welder or these instructions to operate the welder
description: Welders are dangerous in the hands of untrained users

name: Use the welder and accessories in accordance with these instructions, taking into account the working conditions and the work to be performed
description: Use of the welder for operations different from those intended could result in a hazardous situation

name: Do not use the welder for pipe thawing
description: Prohibited use of the welder for pipe thawing operations

name: Maintain welders by checking for misalignment or binding of moving parts, breakage of parts, and any other condition that may affect the welder's operation
description: If damaged, have the welder repaired before use. Many accidents are caused by poorly maintained welders

name: Have your welder serviced by a qualified repair person using only identical replacement parts
description: This will ensure that the Safety of the welder is maintained

name: Maintain labels and nameplates on the Welder
description: These carry important information. If unreadable or missing, contact Harbor Freight Tools for a replacement

name: Unplug before maintenance
description: Unplug the Welder from its electrical outlet before any inspection, maintenance, or cleaning procedures

name: Do not weld on a pressurized or closed cylinder
description: Cylinders can explode when damaged

name: Do not allow an electrode holder, electrode, welding torch, or welding wire to touch the cylinder
description: Gas Shielded Welding - Cylinder Safety precaution

name: Keep cylinders away from any electrical circuits, including welding circuits
description: Gas Shielded Welding - Cylinder Safety precaution

name: Keep protective cap in place over the valve except when the cylinder is in use
description: Gas Shielded Welding - Cylinder Safety precaution

name: Use only correct gas shielding equipment designed specifically for the type of welding you do
description: Maintain this equipment properly. Gas Shielded Welding - Cylinder Safety precaution

name: Protect gas cylinders from heat, being struck, physical damage, slag, flames, sparks, and arcs
description: Gas Shielded Welding - Cylinder Safety precaution

name: Use proper procedures to move cylinders
description: Gas Shielded Welding - Cylinder Safety precaution

name: Save these instructions
description: Operator must retain manual for future reference

name: Proper grounding wire connection to welder grounding system
description: Green wire inside the cord must be connected to the welder's grounding system and must be the only wire connected to the welder's grounding system; never leave grounding wire disconnected or modify power cord plug

name: Tool must be connected to properly grounded outlet
description: Tool must be connected to outlet with same configuration as plug; if outlet type differs, reconnection should be made by qualified service personnel; tool should comply with all local codes and ordinances

name: Do not use extension cord on welder
description: Extension cords must not be used with this welder

name: Use only supplied or identical replacement power cord
description: Use only one of the supplied power cords for this welder or an identical replacement cord

name: Do not install thinner or longer cord on welder
description: Do not install a thinner or longer cord on this welder

name: Do not patch cords together
description: Do not patch cords of any length together as patches may allow moisture to penetrate the insulation, resulting in electric shock

name: Prevent electric shock and death from incorrect grounding
description: To prevent electric shock and death from incorrect grounding wire connection: check with qualified electrician if in doubt about proper grounding; do not use welder if power cord or plug is damaged; have damaged cord or plug repaired by service facility before use; do not use adapter plugs if plug will not fit outlet; have proper outlet installed by qualified electrician

name: Wire feed problems from improper spool unwind direction
description: Tangling and feeding problems that occur when wire spool unwinds clockwise instead of counterclockwise, causing wire to unravel and tangle

name: Wire Spool Unwind Direction
description: Set the wire spool so it will unwind clockwise to prevent wire feed problems, tangling, and feeding issues

name: Spool Spindle Free Rotation Check
description: If wire spool can spin freely, the wingnut is too loose which will cause welding wire to unravel and unspool, causing tangling and feeding problems

name: Gas Connection Leak from Connector Not Fully Inserted

name: Do Not Overtighten Wire Feed Knob

description: Observable problem when welding wire is not held securely with tension during setup

name: Feed Tensioner

name: Wire Tension Setting Guidelines
description: Tension should be 3-5 for solid wire and 2-3 for flux-cored wire; too much force on flux-cored wire will crush it and cause feeding issues

name: Flux-cored wire crushed by excessive tension
description: Excessive force on flux-cored wire during tensioning causes wire crushing and feeding issues

name: Nozzle

name: Contact Tip

description: Procedure to lay the MIG Gun Cable out in a straight line so welding wire moves through it easily, leaving the cover open to observe the feed mechanism

name: Stainless Steel Wire Requires Straight Gun Cable
description: Stainless steel wire is less flexible than other welding wire, making it more difficult to feed through the liner and gun; it is especially important to keep the gun cable straight while feeding stainless steel wire

name: Stainless Steel Wire
description: A type of welding wire that is less flexible than other welding wires and requires special care during setup

description: Diagram showing wire spool, wire inlet liner, feed guide, hold wire securely instruction, idler arm, feed tensioner, nozzle, contact tip, and MIG gun

name: Parts at Welding Voltage - Electric Shock Prevention
description: Keep hands away from Wire Feed mechanism, close door before plugging in unless using Cold Wire Feed, do not touch trigger while feeding wire to prevent electric shock and death

name: Keep hands away from Wire Feed mechanism
description: Hands must be kept away from Wire Feed mechanism to prevent electric shock

name: Close door before plugging in unless using Cold Wire Feed
description: Door must be closed before plugging in the welder unless using Cold Wire Feed to feed wire through to gun

name: Do not touch Trigger while feeding wire through to gun
description: Operator must not touch the trigger while feeding wire through to the gun to prevent accidental arc initiation

name: Unplug Welder and Cool Before Service
description: To prevent serious injury, fire and burns: Unplug the Welder, rest the tool on a heat-proof, electrically non-conductive surface, and allow all parts of the Welder to cool thoroughly before service.

name: Before Each Use Inspection
description: Inspect the general condition of the Welder before each use. Check for: loose hardware, misalignment or binding of moving parts, damaged cord/electrical wiring, frayed or damaged cables, cracked or broken parts, and any other condition that may affect safe operation.

name: Periodic Rear Panel Cleaning
description: Periodically, have a qualified technician remove the Rear Panel and use compressed air to blow out all dust from the interior.

name: Storage After Use
description: After every use, store the welder in a clean and dry location.

name: MIG Gun Nozzle and Contact Tip Inspection and Cleaning
description: For optimal MIG/Flux-Cored wire weld quality, clean and inspect the MIG Gun Contact Tip and Nozzle before each use.

name: Nozzle Removal
description: Pull the Nozzle to remove it.

name: Nozzle Interior Scrubbing
description: Scrub the interior of the Nozzle clean with a wire brush.

name: Nozzle End Examination
description: Examine the end of the Nozzle. The end should be flat and even. If the end is uneven, chipped, melted, cracked, or otherwise damaged, the Nozzle will adversely affect the weld and should be replaced.

name: Contact Tip Unscrew
description: Unscrew the Contact Tip counterclockwise and slide it off the welding wire to remove.

name: Contact Tip Exterior Cleaning
description: Scrub the outside of the Tip clean with a wire brush. Clean out the inside of the tip with a tip cleaner (sold separately). Check that the Tip is the proper type for the wire size used.

name: Contact Tip Hole Examination
description: Examine the shape of the hole at the end of the Contact Tip. It should be an even circle; it should not be oblong or have any bulges in it.

name: Contact Tip Replacement
description: If any problems are noted, replace the Contact Tip. Select a new Tip of the correct size for the welding wire used.

name: Contact Tip and Nozzle Reinstallation
description: Reinstall the Tip and securely reinstall the Nozzle as well.

name: Replacing the LCD Screen Cover
description: Procedure to replace the LCD Screen Cover by carefully prying the frame off the Display and inserting a new Screen Cover into the Screen Frame.

name: Display Frame Removal
description: Carefully pry frame off of Display (22) by inserting flathead screwdriver (sold separately) into one of the side slots.

name: Screen Cover Insertion
description: Insert a new Screen Cover (17) into Screen Frame (18) with the gap facing downward.

name: Screen Frame Reinsertion
description: Insert frame back into slot on Display with the top end being placed in first.
